items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! Saturday, May 17, in Room 112, Bryant Hall. Hundreds of titles for biology, history, and economics. Prices range from $3 to $25. Cash only. Sellers, drop off books Friday afternoon. Doors open 9:00 AM. Arrive early for the best selection!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, how much will the books cost?

options.B: A flat fee of fifteen dollars each
options.C: Ten dollars for any single title

questions.explanation: The flyer states prices range from $3 to $25, which the correct option restates as between three and twenty-five dollars.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a buyer who wants the widest choice of books?

options.A: That person should reserve a title online beforehand
options.B: That person should show up close to opening time
options.C: That person should bring a credit card for payment
options.D: That person should sell some used books first

questions.explanation: The flyer advises arriving early for the best selection, implying that coming near the 9:00 AM opening gives the widest choice.

items.text: FREE FLU SHOTS! No appointment needed. Thursday, October 9, from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM at the Wexler Health Center, second floor. Bring your student ID. Quick and painless. Protect yourself before winter. Walk-ins welcome all day. Stay healthy this season, Wildcats!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what should attendees bring?
questions.explanation: The flyer instructs readers to bring their student ID, which the correct option restates as their student identification card.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about scheduling a visit to the clinic?

options.A: Students must book a slot online
options.C: Visits are limited to morning hours
options.D: Students may arrive without booking ahead

questions.explanation: Because the flyer says no appointment is needed and walk-ins are welcome, it implies students may arrive without booking ahead.

items.text: Hi Marcus, Your replacement student ID is now ready for pickup at the Campus Card Office in Lowell Hall. Please bring $10 in cash to cover the processing fee. The office is open on weekdays until 4:30 PM. Best, Card Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Marcus do to receive his new ID?

options.C: Pay a ten-dollar charge in cash

questions.explanation: The email asks Marcus to bring $10 in cash for the processing fee, which the correct option restates as paying a ten-dollar charge in cash.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about visiting the Campus Card Office?

options.A: It accepts visitors throughout the weekend
options.B: It processes requests entirely by mail
options.C: It stays open late into the evening
options.D: It cannot be visited on a Sunday

questions.explanation: Since the office is open only on weekdays until 4:30 PM, it follows that Marcus cannot visit on a Sunday.

items.text: Dear Olivia, Thank you for volunteering for our memory study. Your session is confirmed for Tuesday, June 3, at 2:00 PM in Room 308, Pearson Hall. You will receive $15 in cash afterward. Please arrive ten minutes early. Best regards, Dr. Reyes

questions.stem: According to the email, what will Olivia receive for participating?

options.B: A voucher for the campus store
options.D: A summary of the study results

questions.explanation: The email states Olivia will receive $15 in cash afterward, which the correct option restates as a fifteen-dollar cash payment.

questions.stem: According to the email, when is Olivia's session scheduled?

options.A: On Tuesday afternoon at two o'clock
options.B: On Monday morning at nine o'clock
options.C: On Thursday evening at six o'clock
options.D: On Wednesday afternoon at four o'clock

questions.explanation: The email confirms the session for Tuesday, June 3, at 2:00 PM, which the correct option restates as Tuesday afternoon at two o'clock.
